task_completion.description: The agent opens the FITS file 'hst_wfpc2_sample.fits' in AstroImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The AstroImageJ main window is visible displaying an astronomical image. The window title or information panel displays the filename 'hst_wfpc2_sample.fits'.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the Multi-Aperture Photometry tool via the Analyze menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Multi-Aperture' settings dialog is open, or the menu path 'Analyze > Aperture Photometry > Multi-Aperture' is highlighted/selected.

task_completion.description: The agent places a photometry aperture on a bright star within the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Aperture rings (typically colored concentric circles) are visible overlaying a bright spot (star) on the image display. Note: The agent should select a visible star; ignore specific coordinate metadata if it conflicts with the image dimensions (200x200).

task_completion.description: The agent generates a measurements table containing photometry data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Measurements' window is visible showing a table with columns like 'Source-C1', 'Source-C2', or 'Rel_flux' and numerical data rows corresponding to the selected star.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the photometry results to the specified folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog or file browser is visible showing the file being saved to the path '~/AstroImages/measurements/' (or 'measurements' folder inside AstroImages).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the AstroImageJ GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the AstroImageJ application windows, menus, and toolbars, rather than a terminal script performing the calculations.

integrity.description: Verify that the measurements table is a native application window.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurements table should look like a standard Java Swing/AWT table (AstroImageJ interface), not a text editor or spreadsheet where data was manually typed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually type flux values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen clicking on stars or using the 'Place Aperture' function, not typing numbers into the measurements grid manually.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The FITS file 'hst_wfpc2_sample.fits' is a 200x200 pixel image. The provided metadata coordinates (400, 400) are invalid (out of bounds). The agent must visually identify and select a bright star (flux > 100 ADU) within the valid 0-199 pixel range. Do not penalize the agent for not using coordinates (400, 400). The maximum flux in the image is approx 3740 ADU.
